text:
St Mary's Hospital 

Institute of Neurology 

Clinic Date: 21 April 2011 

Dr Ahmed  
Greenbrook Health Centre  
145 Cranford Lane, Hounslow TW5 9EX  

Dear Dr Ahmed,  

Michael Turner, DOB: 04-02-1983, Hospital No: H914276 NHS No. 8093172456  
Flat 3, Oakview Supported Living, 12 Beech Road, Hounslow TW4 6JP 

Medication: 

Levetiracetam 1 g twice daily. Staff report good adherence, no notable mood change since the last visit. Rescue midazolam oromucosal 5 mg is available in the care plan but has not been required in the period under review. 

He resides in supported accommodation where rotating staff maintain daily seizure charts and incident logs; copies were available today and used as the primary source alongside his account. He had a cluster of three seizures in Dec (short, not full convulsions, fluctuating awareness, self-terminating). In Feb he had 7 nocturnal seizures, and in Apr a single tonic seizure was recorded during respite care. There were no associated injuries documented. Post-event recovery was within 10–20 minutes on each occasion, and no emergency services were involved. 

Potential triggers noted by staff include poor sleep during room changes and increased anxiety around schedule alterations; caffeine intake has been reduced by the team with no clear effect yet. He has not had recent intercurrent illness. 

Neurological examination today is unchanged. We discussed maintaining consistent sleep routines in the residence and ensuring that new or temporary staff continue the current seizure documentation protocol. I have not altered his medication at this stage. Should the nocturnal events increase in frequency or duration, we would consider a dose optimisation of levetiracetam or alternative strategies after review of the updated charts. 

We will review him again in four months, or earlier if the supported living team identify escalation or prolonged events. 

Yours sincerely

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter provides counts over specific recent months: a cluster of three seizures in December, seven nocturnal seizures in February, and one tonic seizure in April. These are within a 5-month span from December to April (Dec, Jan, Feb, Mar, Apr). Total seizures = 3 + 7 + 1 = 11 over 5 months. No indication of other months with seizures in this period. Therefore frequency = 11 per 5 months. Not seizure-free for 6 months.
